messages.say:
SQL (Structured Query Language) works as a Data Manipulation Language (DML) by allowing users to retrieve, insert, update, and delete data in a database. DML is one of the main components of SQL, used specifically for managing data stored in tables.


Here’s how SQL works as a DML:



🔹 1. SELECT – Retrieve data


Used to fetch data from one or more tables.


SELECT name, age FROM students WHERE age > 20;

This query retrieves names and ages of students older than 20.


🔹 2. INSERT – Add new data


Used to insert new records into a table.


INSERT INTO students (name, age, class) VALUES ('Ali', 21, 'BSCS');

This adds a new student to the students table.


🔹 3. UPDATE – Modify existing data


Used to change data in one or more rows.


UPDATE students SET age = 22 WHERE name = 'Ali';

This updates the age of the student named 'Ali'.


🔹 4. DELETE – Remove data


Used to delete data from a table.


DELETE FROM students WHERE age < 18;

This deletes all students younger than 18.


🔸 Key Points:


DML does not change the structure of the table (that’s done by DDL – Data Definition Language).

These commands usually affect only data in the tables.

DML operations can be rolled back if within a transaction.



If you want, I can also explain DML with a practical example or help you create DML queries for a specific database.


4o
